Here this batch Loads images from your Filepath and passes them into SeedVR if enabled. If not enabled It skips seedvr and allows you to keep originals. 

For this step install Bonds Node suite if you havent: 
git clone https://github.com/bondngn-studios/Bond-Node-Suite/
install under your custom_nodes folder in Comfyui. 

You should install RGTHREE Nodes if you havent search in manager

On the "EnableSeedVRSave" node : This Allows SeedVR to Upscale and Sharpen image. TRUE = SEEDVR, FALSE = ORIGINAL image from Qwen

Step 5 : Upscale/Caption/Remove Meta

For Step 5, look at your picked images folder (Filepath)/foldername
You must enter the total number of photos for SeedVR to process into the RUN number box at top right(Next to RUN). Otherwise it will step through them only 1 at a time. So if you have 15 photos to process and caption, Enter 15 instead of 1

Meta data is through bonds custom node: 
Set Pre-fix ( this will be attached to front of original filename and placed in same location. This can be disabled. 
In Phase 1.5 and Final Node: rgthree displays a compare node from original Qwen image to SeedVR upscaled version. 

NOTE: IF YOU CHOSE "FALSE" on SEEDVR, No image output from seedvr will show in comparer. 

For this Group to function properly You will need to ensure you have QWENVL3 installed

Set all folder locations/Filenames
SET ALL THESE appropriately: 

Filepath:
 where initial images will be stored

Trigger Word will be used on generating captions 

FILENAME: what you want your images to be called

DatasetFolderName: This is the folder where WF will place captioned and scaled Images prepared for LoRA creation. 


NOTE: 

YOU WILL NEED KJNODES (GET/SET)
-------------
IF YOU WANT RESOLUTION BOX YOU WILL NEED PIXORAMA NODES Not required, though allows for better image settings. If you don't want to use it, Disable it and Disable Sized latents from Step 1
-------------

You CAN now bypass Phase 2 NODES
Here We are more or less Repeating Step 5. Enable or disable SeedVR first. 
Cherry Pick your images to keep really only need 15 - 20 more since we already have face lock images. 

After finding best images, RE-ENABLE STEP 1.5 + Batch
this means Reenable Step 5 and Step 4
Disable Step 8.

This step is Generating your LoRA using the newly created DATASET images + Captions. Upload BOTH IMAGES + CAPTIONS into Ostris
---------------------------------------------------
IMPORTANT: DELETE THE ORIGINAL IMAGES OUT OF YOUR FILEPATH (not dataset + captions) the original filepath or put them somewhere else. We already have these, and do not want them being duplicates in the folder for Last Dataset creation piece. 
----------------------------------------------------
ALSO CHANGE THE NUMBER NEXT TO RUN BUTTON BACK TO 1. 
----------------------------------------------------

Sometimes QWENVL gets it wrong, validate your captions for triggerword and remove any extra "identity" words (hair color, eye color, etc.) these should be covered by the "triggerword" 

SUGGESTED: TRAIN in whatever you want, however, I strongly suggest choosing krea2Turbo. Its Fast and doesn't take a lot of vram. 

watch your LoRA around 1200 - 1800 this is generally the area I have found that sparks the identity. 

BONUS: FULL INCLUDED CONFIG FILE FOR OSTRIS AI. Change the name, trigger_word: ""
dataset, and just change triggerword in samples or create your own.(NEAR BOTTOM)
If you open the side panel to copy the details, it has correct spacing. in comfy (TOP RIGHT CORNER)
Side note: when using a pasted template, I have found its best to paste this in config, then go out select a different Model, then reselect Krea2Turbo from dropdown, for some reason it likes to choose anima randomly

---
job: "extension"
config:
  name: "karma_krea2_identityshift"
  process:
    - type: "diffusion_trainer"
      training_folder: "/workspace/ai-toolkit/output"
      sqlite_db_path: "./aitk_db.db"
      device: "cuda"
      trigger_word: "k@rma"
      performance_log_every: 10
      network:
        type: "lora"
        linear: 32
        linear_alpha: 32
        lokr_full_rank: true
        lokr_factor: -1
        network_kwargs:
          ignore_if_contains: []
      save:
        dtype: "bf16"
        save_every: 200
        max_step_saves_to_keep: 4
        save_format: "diffusers"
        push_to_hub: false
      datasets:
        - folder_path: "/workspace/ai-toolkit/datasets/karma"
          mask_path: null
          mask_min_value: 0.1
          default_caption: ""
          caption_ext: "txt"
          caption_dropout_rate: 0.05
          cache_latents_to_disk: false
          is_reg: false
          network_weight: 1
          resolution:
            - 512
            - 768
            - 1024
          controls: []
          shrink_video_to_frames: true
          num_frames: 1
          flip_x: false
          flip_y: false
          num_repeats: 1
      train:
        batch_size: 1
        bypass_guidance_embedding: false
        steps: 2000
        gradient_accumulation: 1
        train_unet: true
        train_text_encoder: false
        gradient_checkpointing: true
        noise_scheduler: "flowmatch"
        optimizer: "adamw8bit"
        timestep_type: "linear"
        content_or_style: "balanced"
        optimizer_params:
          weight_decay: 0.0001
        unload_text_encoder: false
        cache_text_embeddings: false
        lr: 0.0001
        ema_config:
          use_ema: false
          ema_decay: 0.99
        skip_first_sample: false
        force_first_sample: false
        disable_sampling: false
        dtype: "bf16"
        diff_output_preservation: false
        diff_output_preservation_multiplier: 1
        diff_output_preservation_class: "person"
        switch_boundary_every: 1
        loss_type: "mse"
      logging:
        log_every: 1
        use_ui_logger: true
      model:
        name_or_path: "krea/Krea-2-Turbo"
        quantize: true
        qtype: "qfloat8"
        quantize_te: true
        qtype_te: "qfloat8"
        arch: "krea2:turbo"
        low_vram: true
        model_kwargs: {}
        compile: false
        layer_offloading: false
        layer_offloading_text_encoder_percent: 1
        layer_offloading_transformer_percent: 1
        assistant_lora_path: "ostris/krea2_turbo_training_adapter/krea2_turbo_training_adapter_v1.safetensors"
      sample:
        sampler: "flowmatch"
        sample_every: 200
        width: 1024
        height: 1024
        samples:
          - prompt: "k@rma, bustier and thong, arms raised, full body shot, smiling confidently, warm candlelight, oiled skin"
          - prompt: "k@rma, holding a coffee cup, white almost transparent button up shirt with no bra underneath, tight against her chest, in a beanie, sitting at a cafe"
          - prompt: "k@rma, fishnet stockings and garter, kneeling, high angle shot, smiling confidently, warm candlelight"
          - prompt: "k@rma, nude with hands covering, touching hair, low angle shot, smiling confidently, harsh shadows, arching back"
          - prompt: "k@rma,  micro bikini, standing, side view, aroused expression, golden hour sunlight, posing in mirror"
          - prompt: "k@rma, back-right quarter view with head turned slightly toward camera,slight low-angle shot,close-up portrait,confident slight smirk,casual flannel shirt over a tee,urban sidewalk"
        neg: ""
        seed: 42
        walk_seed: true
        guidance_scale: 1
        sample_steps: 9
        num_frames: 1
        fps: 1
meta:
  name: "[name]"
  version: "1.0"

You are a professional LoRA dataset captioner specializing in realistic character training for KREA2.

Analyze the provided image and generate one concise, accurate training caption.

The caption must begin with the trigger word:

[TRIGGER_WORD],

Caption the visible content in this order:

1. Trigger word
2. Shot type and camera angle
3. Pose or body position
4. Facial expression
5. Clothing and visible accessories
6. Lighting
7. Background or environment
8. Important visible character details that must be learned by the LoRA

Caption only what is clearly visible in the image.

Keep the caption natural, descriptive, and between 25 and 60 words.

For identity LoRA training:

* Do not describe permanent identity traits that should be learned through the trigger word unless the trait is especially important and appears inconsistently across the dataset.
* Do not repeatedly describe hair color, eye color, skin tone, facial structure, ethnicity, age, or body measurements.
* Do include important persistent markings such as scars, tattoos, unusual anatomy, or distinctive features when they are clearly visible.
* Describe hair styling only when it changes between images.
* Describe makeup only when it is clearly visible or different from the character’s normal appearance.
* Describe clothing precisely so the LoRA does not permanently associate one outfit with the character.
* Describe the framing accurately, such as extreme close-up, headshot, chest-up portrait, waist-up shot, three-quarter body, or full-body shot.
* Include left profile, right profile, rear view, low angle, high angle, or three-quarter angle when applicable.
* Do not use vague quality tags such as masterpiece, best quality, amazing, beautiful, stunning, 8K, or ultra-detailed.
* Do not invent hidden details.
* Do not mention that the image is AI-generated.
* Do not include opinions or subjective attractiveness descriptions.
* Do not include quotation marks.
* Do not include headings, explanations, bullet points, or multiple caption options.
* Return only the final caption as one line of plain text.

Example output:

k@rma, chest-up portrait photographed from a slight left three-quarter angle, sitting upright on a living room couch with a relaxed neutral expression, wearing a loose cropped gray tank top and delicate necklace, soft daylight entering from a nearby window, softly blurred residential interior in the background
